name: Guatemalan Desayuno Chapin

ingredients.ingredient: Frijoles rojos volteados (Refried beans, red, canned)

steps: Fry the eggs to your liking.
Warm the refried beans and plantain.
Serve together with the queso fresco and warm tortillas.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Frijoles rojos volteados (Refried beans, red, canned)

steps_detailed: Fry the eggs in a little oil to your liking.
Warm the refried beans and the baked plantain.
Serve everything together with the queso fresco, a dollop of cream (standing in for crema), and warm tortillas - a classic Guatemalan breakfast plate, chapin being a common nickname for Guatemalans.

name: Guatemalan Mosh

ingredients.ingredient: Oats (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps: Simmer the oats in the milk with the cinnamon for 6-8 minutes until thick and creamy.
Serve warm.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Oats (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Simmer the oats in the milk with the cinnamon over medium-low heat, stirring often, 8-10 minutes until thick and creamy.
Stir in the sugar and serve warm - mosh, a cinnamon oatmeal, is a common, comforting Guatemalan breakfast, especially for children.

name: Guatemalan Atol de Elote

ingredients.ingredient: Corn, sweet, yellow, canned, drained solids, rinsed with tap water

steps: Blend the corn with the milk until smooth.
Simmer with the sugar for 8-10 minutes, stirring, until thickened.
Serve warm.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Corn, sweet, yellow, canned, drained solids, rinsed with tap water

steps_detailed: Blend the corn with the milk until very smooth, straining if a finer texture is wanted.
Simmer with the sugar and cinnamon over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, 10-12 minutes until thick and warming.
Serve warm - atol de elote, a sweet corn drink, is a beloved Guatemalan breakfast and street-food staple.

name: Guatemalan Frijoles Volteados

ingredients.ingredient: Frijoles rojos volteados (Refried beans, red, canned)

steps: Warm the refried beans in the oil, mashing smooth.
Top with the crumbled queso fresco.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Frijoles rojos volteados (Refried beans, red, canned)

steps_detailed: Saute the onion in the oil until soft, then add the beans and mash until smooth and glossy, cooking 5-6 minutes.
Top with the crumbled queso fresco - volteado means 'flipped,' referring to the traditional technique of cooking the beans until they hold together enough to flip like a pancake.

name: Guatemalan Jocon
steps: Brown the chicken with the onion.
Blend the cilantro and cabbage with a splash of water into a bright green sauce.
Add the sauce to the chicken and simmer 20-25 minutes until tender.
steps_detailed: Brown the chicken with the onion and garlic over medium-high heat, 8-10 minutes.
Blend the cilantro, cabbage (standing in for tomatillo), and jalapeno with a splash of water into a smooth, bright green sauce.
Add the sauce to the chicken and simmer, covered, 22-28 minutes until tender - jocon's vivid green color and herbal brightness make it one of Guatemala's most distinctive stews.

name: Guatemalan Caldo de Res

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Simmer the beef in water until nearly tender, about 45 minutes.
Add the potato, carrot, and cabbage, and simmer 20 more minutes.
Serve hot with rice.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps_detailed: Simmer the beef with the onion in 3 cups water for 45-50 minutes until nearly tender.
Add the potato and carrot, and simmer 15 minutes.
Add the cabbage and continue simmering 10-12 minutes until all the vegetables are tender.
Top with cilantro and serve hot with rice - a hearty, everyday Guatemalan beef soup.

name: Guatemalan Hilachas

ingredients.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps: Simmer the beef until very tender, then shred finely.
Simmer the shredded beef with the tomatoes, potato, and onion for 20-25 minutes until the potato is tender.
Serve with rice.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Beef, chuck, short ribs, boneless, separable lean and fat, trimmed to 0" fat, all grades, raw

steps_detailed: Simmer the beef in water until completely tender, 1.5-2 hours, then shred it finely, meaning 'shredded threads.'
Saute the onion, bell pepper, and garlic until softened, then add the tomatoes, shredded beef, and potato with a splash of the cooking broth.
Simmer 22-28 minutes until the potato is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Serve with rice - hilachas is a beloved everyday Guatemalan comfort dish.

name: Guatemalan Pepian de Pollo
steps: Simmer the chicken thigh in water 20 minutes until cooked through; reserve the broth.
Toast the pumpkin seeds in a dry pan until they pop, then blend with the tomatoes and a splash of broth into a sauce.
Simmer the sauce with the shredded chicken 10 minutes. Serve over the cooked rice.
steps_detailed: Simmer the chicken with the onion in water 22-28 minutes until cooked through; reserve the broth and shred the chicken.
Toast the pumpkin seeds and sesame seeds in a dry pan until fragrant and the pumpkin seeds pop, then blend with the tomatoes and a splash of the reserved broth into a rich, nutty sauce.
Simmer the sauce with the shredded chicken 10-12 minutes to meld the flavors.
Serve over the cooked rice - pepian, thickened with toasted seeds rather than flour, is widely considered Guatemala's national dish.

name: Guatemalan Tamales Colorados
steps: Whisk the masa harina with warm water and the achiote paste into a thick, spreadable dough.
Simmer the chicken with the diced tomatoes 15 minutes until cooked; shred and stir into the dough.
Spread the filled dough onto a piece of parchment or a soaked corn husk, fold into a packet, and steam 45 minutes.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Raisins, dark, seedless (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Whisk the masa harina with warm water, the oil, and the achiote paste into a thick, smooth, spreadable dough.
Simmer the chicken with the diced tomatoes 15-18 minutes until cooked through; shred and mix with a portion of the tomato sauce, folding in the raisins.
Spread a layer of the masa dough onto parchment or a soaked corn husk, top with the chicken filling, fold into a sealed packet, and steam 45-55 minutes until firm.
Tamales colorados, named for their red achiote-tinted dough, are a Guatemalan holiday centerpiece, especially at Christmas.

name: Guatemalan Chiles Rellenos
steps: Roast the peppers until softened.
Brown the beef and stuff into the peppers.
Dip in the beaten egg and flour, and pan-fry until golden on all sides.
steps_detailed: Roast the whole peppers at 425F (220C) for 15-18 minutes until softened and lightly charred, then carefully remove the seeds through a slit.
Brown the beef with the onion, and stuff generously into the peppers.
Dip each stuffed pepper in the flour, then the beaten egg, and pan-fry in the oil, turning, 4-6 minutes until golden on all sides.
A festive Guatemalan dish, often part of larger holiday spreads.

name: Guatemalan Kak'ik
steps: Brown the chicken with the paprika.
Add the tomatoes and a splash of water, and simmer 25-30 minutes until tender.
Stir in the cilantro before serving with rice.
steps_detailed: Brown the chicken with the paprika, garlic, and jalapeno over medium-high heat, 8-10 minutes.
Add the diced tomatoes and 1 cup water, and simmer, covered, 28-35 minutes until tender and the broth is richly flavored.

Stir in the cilantro just before serving with rice - kak'ik, traditionally made with turkey and rooted in Q'eqchi' Mayan cuisine, is one of Guatemala's most historically significant dishes, recognized by UNESCO.

name: Guatemalan Chuchitos
steps: Whisk the masa harina with warm water and achiote into a thick dough.
Brown the beef with the tomatoes, then fold a little into the dough.
Wrap small portions in corn husk or parchment and steam 30-35 minutes.
steps_detailed: Whisk the masa harina with warm water and the achiote paste into a thick, spreadable dough.
Brown the beef with the onion and tomatoes, 8-10 minutes, then fold a spoonful into each portion of dough.
Wrap small portions tightly in corn husk or parchment and steam 30-40 minutes until firm.
Chuchitos are a smaller, simpler cousin of tamales, a beloved everyday Guatemalan snack.

name: Guatemalan Enchiladas Guatemaltecas

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Cassava Flour Grain Free Tortillas, Cassava Flour
ingredients.note: tortilla, fried flat, as a tostada base

steps: Fry the tortilla flat and crisp.
Brown the beef and pile onto the tortilla with the cabbage and queso fresco.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Cassava Flour Grain Free Tortillas, Cassava Flour
ingredients_full.note: tortilla, fried flat, as a tostada base

steps_detailed: Fry the tortilla flat in a little oil until crisp, forming a tostada-like base.
Brown the beef, then pile onto the crisp tortilla with the shredded cabbage, tomatoes, queso fresco, and sliced egg.
Unlike Mexican enchiladas, Guatemalan enchiladas are open-faced and eaten like a loaded tostada - a popular festive snack.

name: Guatemalan Fried Plantains
steps: Slice the plantain and fry in the oil until golden, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Serve warm.

ingredients_full.note: as a crema stand-in, to serve

steps_detailed: Slice the plantain into thick rounds and fry in the oil over medium heat, 4-5 minutes per side, until deeply golden and caramelized.
Serve warm with a dollop of cream - fried sweet plantains are a beloved Guatemalan side and snack.

name: Guatemalan Tostadas

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Cassava Flour Grain Free Tortillas, Cassava Flour
Frijoles rojos volteados (Refried beans, red, canned)

steps: Fry the tortilla until crisp and flat.
Spread with the refried beans and top with the cabbage and queso fresco.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Cassava Flour Grain Free Tortillas, Cassava Flour
Frijoles rojos volteados (Refried beans, red, canned)

steps_detailed: Fry the tortilla in a little oil until crisp and flat, forming a sturdy base.
Spread generously with the refried beans, then top with the shredded cabbage, diced tomatoes, and crumbled queso fresco.
Guatemalan tostadas, sold by street vendors everywhere, come with countless topping variations.

name: Guatemalan Rellenitos de Platano

ingredients.note: mashed, as a black bean stand-in

steps: Mash the plantain into a smooth dough.
Sweeten the mashed beans with the sugar.
Wrap the bean filling in a portion of plantain dough, shape into an oval, and fry until golden.

ingredients_full.note: mashed, as a black bean stand-in

steps_detailed: Mash the plantain until smooth and dough-like.
Mash the beans with the sugar and cinnamon until sweet and paste-like.
Take a portion of the plantain dough, flatten, spoon the bean filling into the center, and fold closed into a small oval.
Fry in the oil over medium heat, turning, 4-6 minutes until deeply golden on all sides.
Rellenitos, sweet plantain fritters filled with sweetened beans, are a treasured Guatemalan dessert.

name: Guatemalan Poe
steps: Simmer the rice in the coconut milk with the sugar and cinnamon for 20-25 minutes until thick and pudding-like.
Serve warm or chilled.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Raisins, dark, seedless (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Simmer the rice in the coconut milk with the sugar, cinnamon, and raisins over medium-low heat, stirring often, 22-28 minutes until thick and creamy.
Serve warm or chilled - poe, a coconut rice pudding from the Garifuna and Caribbean coastal tradition, is enjoyed in Guatemala's Caribbean region as well as its neighbors.

name: Guatemalan Torrejas

ingredients.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Graham Crackers Flavored With Honey, Honey

steps: Dip the crackers in the beaten egg.
Fry until golden on both sides.
Warm the sugar with a splash of water and the cinnamon into a light syrup, and pour over.

ingredients_full.ingredient: 365 (Whole Foods) Graham Crackers Flavored With Honey, Honey

steps_detailed: Dip the crackers (standing in for slightly stale bread) in the beaten egg, coating well.
Fry in the oil over medium heat, 2-3 minutes per side, until golden.
Warm the sugar with a splash of water and the cinnamon over low heat 3-4 minutes into a light syrup, and pour generously over the fried torrejas.
Torrejas, a Guatemalan take on French toast, are especially associated with Lent and Holy Week.

name: Guatemalan Chirmol
steps: Combine the tomatoes, onion, cilantro, and lime juice.
Season with salt and serve alongside grilled meats.
steps_detailed: Combine the tomatoes, onion, cilantro, lime juice, and jalapeno.
Season with salt and let sit 10 minutes so the flavors meld - chirmol is a simple, bright Guatemalan table sauce, served with nearly everything grilled.

name: Guatemalan Salsa de Ajonjoli
steps: Toast the sesame seeds until golden.
Blend with the tomatoes and garlic into a smooth sauce.
Warm gently before serving.
steps_detailed: Toast the sesame seeds in a dry pan until golden and fragrant, 2-3 minutes.
Blend with the tomatoes, garlic, and oil into a smooth, nutty sauce.
Warm gently before serving over grilled chicken or vegetables - a simpler cousin of pepian's toasted-seed sauce tradition.
